h2: 2. Complete your forms and gather your documents

sections.heading: Read the instruction guide
items: The instruction guide will help you complete the application correctly. It includes information about
Ask for this guide in another format

sections.heading: 1. Check your eligibility
items: Before you start the application, check if you're eligible .

sections.heading: 2. Complete your forms and gather your documents
items: Make sure you fill out all the forms and documents that are listed in your document checklist (IMM 0203) (opens in a new tab) . You must fill out and print the following documents:
Get help to open an application form or to fill one out in the Help Centre.
bullets: Generic Application Form for Canada (IMM 0008) (opens in a new tab) Under “Program under which you are applying,” select Other Under “Category under which you are applying,” select In-Canada - Humanitarian & Compassionate Considerations
Under “Program under which you are applying,” select Other
Under “Category under which you are applying,” select In-Canada - Humanitarian & Compassionate Considerations
Schedule A - Background/Declaration (IMM 5669) (opens in a new tab)
Additional Family Information (IMM 5406) (opens in a new tab) , if applicable
completed document checklist (check all the forms and documents you have included)

sections.heading: Photo specifications
items: You need one photo for each person on your application.
Show the photo specifications to your photographer to make sure your photos meet our requirements.

sections.heading: 3. Mail your application
items: Send the complete application in a stamped envelope. If you are applying as a family , send all the documents in one envelope.
You must also write your name and address in the top left-hand corner of the envelope.
Mail the application to the following address:
Mailing address IRCC - Humanitarian Migration Vancouver FN/State Care 300-800 Burrard St Vancouver BC V6Z 0B6

sections.heading: Track your application
items: If you submit a paper application, we recommend that you use a postal or courier service with a tracking number to ensure you get proof that your application was delivered to the IRCC office.
Find out how to check if your application has been received .
